data.title: Stock index futures as traders assess the latest labor report; eyes on Middle East tensions
data.description:
Stock index futures rose on Wednesday as traders continued to monitor developments in the Middle East and digested the better-than-expected labor report.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) edged higher by +0.4%. Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.5%, and Dow futures (INDU) +0.3%.

U.S. private sector employment grew by 63K jobs in February, topping the +43K consensus and accelerating from the 11K added in January, according to data released by ADP on Wednesday.

Oil inched lower, with crude oil futures (CL1:COM) down 1.22% to $73.65.

U.S. Treasury yields moved higher across the curve. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 1.6 basis points to 4.09%, while the 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) advanced 3.5 basis points to 3.54%. The 30-year Treasury yield (US30Y) moved up 2.4 basis points to 4.73%.

Top gainers in premarket trading included Coinbase Global (COIN) +5.86%, Ross Stores (ROST) +5.78%, and Moderna (MRNA) +4.56%. Decliners included Live Nation Entertainment (LYV) -3.68%, Gen Digital (GEN) -1.96%, and Principal Financial Group (PFG) -1.65%.

data.title: Apollo warns Strait of Hormuz disruption could rattle global energy supply
data.description:
Heightened geopolitical tensions in the Middle East are drawing renewed attention to the strategic importance of the Strait of Hormuz, a narrow waterway that plays a central role in global energy flows.

According to analysis from Torsten Slok of Apollo, the passage remains one of the world’s most critical oil chokepoints. Roughly 20.9 million barrels per day of petroleum liquids move through the strait, making it the second-largest oil transit corridor globally, behind only the Strait of Malacca, which handles about 23.7 million barrels per day. 

Other major transit routes carry significantly smaller volumes, including the Suez Canal at 8.8 million barrels per day and the Bab el-Mandeb at 8.6 million barrels per day. 

Because such a large share of global crude exports flows through Hormuz, any disruption could quickly ripple through energy markets, tightening supply and pushing prices higher. 

Despite escalating conflict involving Iran, Israel, and the United States, oil prices were modestly lower on Wednesday, with crude (CL1:COM) slipping about 0.5%, marking the first pullback since the start of the conflict. Investors remain highly sensitive to developments that could threaten the critical shipping lane.

See the below chart provided by Apollo:

Energy ETFs: (XLE), (AMLP), (VDE), (XOP), (OIH), and (IXC).

Oil ETFs: (USO), (UCO), (DBO), (OILK), and (USL).

Natural Gas ETFs: (UNG), (BOIL), and (UNL).

data.title: Bridger Aerospace names Adolphus Andrews as new COO
data.description:
• Bridger Aerospace (BAER) has appointed Adolphus Andrews as Chief Operating Officer, effective March 2.
  • Andrews will oversee all operational functions across Bridger Aerospace’s fleet, maintenance, and mission support organizations, with a focus on enhancing readiness, operational excellence, safety, and scalable growth to meet increasing demand for aerial firefighting and mission-critical services, the company said.
  • Previously, Andrews held senior leadership roles at Lockheed Martin. He has also served in the U.S. Air Force and Air National Guard 
  • BAER +2.93% premarket to $2.46.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Genius Sports shares underwater as another quarterly loss spooks investors
data.description:
Driven by strong performance in its Betting and Media segment and contributions from recent acquisitions, Genius Sports (GENI) delivered 37% revenue growth and a 49% increase in adjusted EBITDA, significantly narrowing its quarterly loss.

Despite those gains, shares fell in premarket trading Wednesday as investors reacted to non-recurring costs, a 15% decline in Sports Technology and Services revenue, and another quarterly net loss.

Combined revenue for all segments increased 31% during the fourth quarter to $240.5M, $5.5M better than expected and helped narrow the company’s net loss per share by a comparable amount to $0.08 per share. This, however, was worse than expected as analysts expected the company to swing to a profit of $0.03 per share.

This was a result of increased costs for research and development (+ 71%), sales and marketing (+70%), and general and administrative (+57%), in addition to “transaction expenses” of $5M.

For FY26, Genius Sports (GENI) expects to generate combined revenue of approximately $810M to 820M (versus $800.4M consensus) and adjusted EBITDA of approximately $180M to $190M. This implies year-over-year group revenue and adjusted EBITDA growth of 22% and 36%, respectively, at the midpoint.

data.title: Scandium Canada upsizes bought deal LIFE offering of units to $15 million
data.description:
• Scandium Canada (SCD:CA) will increase the size of its bought-deal offering to sell 68.18M units of the company at a price per nit of C$0.22 for aggregate gross proceeds to the company of C$15M.
  • Each unit will consist of one common share in the capital of the company and one common share purchase warrant of the company.
  • Each warrant will entitle the holder thereof to acquire one common share in the capital of the company at a price per warrant share of C$0.30 for a period of 30 months from the closing date.
  • The net proceeds from the sale of the units will be used by the company for (i) project expenditures and works on the Crater Lake project; (ii) development and pre-commercialization work on the Company's proprietary Al Sc alloys; and (iii) for general corporate and working capital purposes.
  • The oﬀering is expected to close on or about March 17, 2026.
  • TMXXF -0.31% after hours to $35.0208.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: U.S. shale drillers unlikely to boost production despite oil rally - report
data.description:
U.S. shale drillers can't increase production quickly enough to offset oil supply disruptions caused by the U.S.-Israel-Iran conflict, industry experts told the Financial Times.

Shale producers would resist spending more on drilling until they were certain that oil prices would remain high, according to industry veteran Scott Sheffield.

He noted that the recent oil rally would give shale producers extra cash flow, which they would likely use to reduce debt, conduct buybacks, and pay dividends. 

"But once the war ends, then it's gonna fall back pretty quickly," Sheffield warned. "Also, you got to remember the companies are running out of [drilling] inventory… I do not anticipate anybody adding any rigs."

Kirk Edwards, president of Latigo Petroleum — an independent producer that operates in the Permian basin, said it's too early to make a move. "What Permian producers need in my opinion is a stable $75 price… over the next 12 months."

The Energy Information Administration last month forecast that U.S. crude oil production would be 13.6M bbl/d in 2026, unchanged from 2025, and then go down to 13.3M bbl/d in 2027.

Reversing that trend would take more time. "U.S. shale could respond, but incremental supply would require several months given drilling, completion and infrastructure lead times," JPMorgan analyst Natasha Kaneva said.

data.title: Novo Nordisk gets another FDA warning over GLP-1 ad claims
data.description:
The FDA has warned Novo Nordisk (NVO) for making “false or misleading” claims in a TV advertisement for its diabetes therapy Ozempic, the second time in less than a month the Danish drugmaker has come under U.S. regulatory scrutiny over its promotional campaigns related to the GLP-1 product.

In a letter dated Feb. 26, the agency stated that Novo Nordisk's (NVO) commercial, “There’s Only One Ozempic,” misrepresented the injectable’s approved uses and suggested, without sufficient evidence, that the drug was better than other GLP-1s approved for Type 2 diabetes.

The FDA added that the advertisement, featuring actor Justin Long and comedian John Hodgman, implied that all patients with type 2 diabetes are eligible for all of Ozempic’s indications, even though they must have other conditions that make them candidates for the therapy.

“We take all regulatory feedback seriously and are in the process of responding to the FDA to address their concerns regarding the advertisement’s presentation,” Novo (NVO) spokesperson Liz Skrbkova told Bloomberg after the FDA published the letter on Tuesday.

The FDA called on the company to “take immediate action to address any violations,” including plans to cease the airing of the ad or potentially halt the distribution of Ozempic. Novo (NVO) has 15 days to submit a written response to the agency.

Novo (NVO) received a similar warning letter from the FDA in early February for making “misleading” claims in a TV commercial for its recently launched oral weight loss therapy, the Wegovy pill, which contains semaglutide, the active pharmaceutical ingredient of Ozempic.

data.title: Dycom Industries jumps after record Q4 results, tops Street revenue, EPS views
data.description:
Dycom Industries (DY) rose 6.1% in premarket trading Wednesday after reporting record fiscal fourth-quarter results and issuing a fiscal 2027 outlook that calls for continued growth and margin expansion. Its stock is up 151% in the past 12 months through Tuesday’s close.

Q4 revenue and earnings beat estimates

Dycom (DY) said contract revenues rose 34.4% to $1.46 billion, above the Wall Street consensus estimate of $1.35 billion. Organic contract revenues increased 16.6%, excluding acquired revenues and the extra week in the company’s 53-week fiscal year.

Adjusted earnings were $2.03 a share, topping estimates for $1.78 a share. Dycom (DY) reported adjusted net income of $60.5 million.

Net income fell to $16.3 million, or $0.55 a share, from $32.7 million, or $1.11 a share, a year earlier.

Margins, cash flow and backlog

Dycom (DY) posted adjusted earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization of $162.4 million, up from $116.4 million a year earlier. Adjusted ebitda margin was 11.1% of contract revenues, compared with 10.7% a year earlier.

Operating cash flow was $419.0 million and free cash flow was $367.1 million for the quarter. Total backlog rose to $9.542 billion from $7.760 billion a year earlier.

Acquisition, segments and outlook

Dycom (DY) said it completed the acquisition of Power Solutions on December 23, expanding into electrical infrastructure work for data centers and other critical facilities. It also updated its reporting to two segments: communications and building systems.

For fiscal 2027, Dycom (DY) forecast contract revenues of $6.85 billion to $7.15 billion, compared with the consensus estimate of $6.88 billion. For the fiscal first quarter, the company projected contract revenues of $1.64 billion to $1.71 billion and adjusted ebitda of $202 million to $218 million.

Dycom (DY) President and Chief Executive Dan Peyovich said in a statement, “Our strong fourth quarter performance closed a record year for Dycom, with ramping organic growth, meaningful margin expansion and increased free cash flow.”

data.title: U.S. private sector adds 63K jobs in February, higher than expected: ADP jobs report
data.description:
February U.S. private sector employment: +63K vs. +43K consensus and +11K prior (revised from +22K), according to data released by ADP on Wednesday.

Pay: +4.5% vs. +4.5% in January.

Developing… Check back for updates.

data.title: Ocugen GAAP EPS of -$0.06 in-line
data.description:
• Ocugen press release (OCGN): Q4 GAAP EPS of -$0.06 in-line.
  • Ocugen’s cash and restricted cash, totalled $18.9 million as of December 31, 2025, compared to $58.8 million as of December 31, 2024.
  • The Company estimates that additional proceeds from the $22.5 million financing in January 2026 will enable it to fund its operations into the fourth quarter of 2026. If the Janus Henderson warrants are fully exercised this year, it is expected that cash runway will be extended into the second quarter of 2027.
